task_completion.description: Agent selects the source DICOM directory for import.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file selection dialog, import screen, or terminal command showing the path /home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium being accessed or selected.

task_completion.description: The CT Cranium DICOM series is successfully loaded into the medical imaging application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the CT Cranium scan (skull/head) displayed in the application's 2D (axial, sagittal, coronal) or 3D viewports.

task_completion.description: Agent applies a rotation to reorient the volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for active rotation tools, transform dialogs, or a visible change in the orientation/tilt of the skull in the multi-planar reconstruction (MPR) views or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the export of the reformatted DICOM series to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Export', 'Save As', or similar dialog where the destination path is explicitly set to /home/ga/Documents/reoriented_dicom.

task_completion.description: The reformatted DICOM series is successfully exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export success notification, a completed progress bar, or a file manager/terminal window showing multiple exported files inside /home/ga/Documents/reoriented_dicom.

integrity.description: Agent used a graphical medical imaging application (e.g., InVesalius) or appropriate tools to process the files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify the agent interacts with a GUI application to load, rotate, and export the DICOM series, rather than just copying files.

integrity.description: The rotation was genuinely applied to the volume.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the rotation is performed using the application's built-in tools and the visual orientation of the scan actually changes.

integrity.description: The exported files are generated through the application's export feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify the agent uses the software's export functionality rather than simply copying the original DICOM files to the new directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source DICOM directory is /home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium. The output directory must be /home/ga/Documents/reoriented_dicom. A successful export should result in more than 50 files in the output directory.
